danger_signals.visual_form: Visible enemy craft in 3D space; polygon models rendering on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats requiring evasion or destruction

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, distinctly designed enemy craft at planet endpoints
danger_signals.meaning: Critical mission checkpoint; major threat requiring defeat to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Rendered terrain geometry, tunnel structures, environmental obstacles
danger_signals.meaning: Physical danger to player craft; collision risk during flight

danger_signals.visual_form: Weapon fire effects, missile trails, energy effects on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat situation requiring tactical response

safety_signals.visual_form: Navigation waypoints, marked target locations in mission objectives
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage markers; established navigation route

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated endpoint location marked in mission parameters
Absence of countdown timer or time-limit visual indicator
safety_signals.meaning: Player can explore safely without urgency-induced danger

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player-piloted TV-202 starfighter; mission objectives centered on Earth defense

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinctive enemy spacecraft designs; visually differentiated from player craft
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy craft spawn across multiple planets (9 level environments); fleet presence indicates faction control of space

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Supercomputer representation on Proxima Seven; final boss entity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Located on Proxima Seven; central to war narrative revelation in Episode 3

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: ASFAR fleet presence across multiple planets; enemy spacecraft rendering throughout missions; combat scenarios as default state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must destroy enemy targets; hostile NPCs engage on sight; mission structure revolves around enemy elimination

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Nine distinct planetary environments rendered as separate level stages; each planet contains enemy forces and mission objectives
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Each planet features boss enemy at endpoint requiring defeat before progression; mission objectives include tunnel navigation and checkpoint clearing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Proxima Seven environment; supercomputer entity as final boss visual; hidden planet bonus content
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final mission objective shifts to eliminating X.I.; hidden mission reveals planet metamorphosis caused by external force

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Open space within mission parameters; multiple pathway options including secret tunnels; varied terrain types across levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No time limit indicator; player can explore environments at variable speeds; multiple objective types available (combat, navigation, checkpoint, extraction)

environmental_storytelling.example: Nine planetary environments across three episodes communicate expanding conflict; each planet contains environmental variety (terrain, tunnels, open space) revealing world geography and occupation state
Enemy destruction targets, tunnel navigation, checkpoint markers, and extraction points communicate tactical situation; boss enemies at planet endpoints mark territorial control points
Hidden tunnels, hidden ships, and bonus hidden planet (CD-ROM version) communicate world depth; player must explore to uncover narrative revelations and alternative pathways
Episode 3 discovery that X.I. supercomputer controls ASFAR and war; hidden mission reveals X.I. drove individual (Sy Wickens) to insanity; story unfolds through mission completion and exploration

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can select from seven weapon types; can activate afterburners for high-speed evasion at cost of fire capability; maintains flight stability with no inertia physics allowing instant direction changes
Engage player on sight; render as distinct visual models; boss variants appear at critical mission points with increased threat level
Can survive multiple hits; tolerates some terrain collisions including tunnel navigation; indicates persistent threat tolerance rather than one-hit-death fragility
Terrain geometry and tunnel structures present navigation challenges; collisionable but survivable; communicate level design complexity without instant kill mechanics

notes: Terminal Velocity (1995) communicates world-state primarily through combat encounter design, planetary progression, and mission objective structure rather than explicit visual faction markers. The game uses environmental variety and mission endpoints (boss encounters) to signal political territory control and conflict escalation. Narrative revelations about X.I. supercomputer control are integrated through mission progression and hidden content, supporting thematic exploration of hidden truth beneath apparent conflict. The absence of time-pressure mechanics and presence of exploration options (secret tunnels, hidden ships) communicate player agency within conflict narrative.
